data.title: Horizon Educational Reports Lower Fiscal H1 Profit, Revenue
data.description:
Horizon Educational (SASE:9603) said Sunday that profit and revenue for the first half of fiscal 2025 declined year over year.

Net profit for the six months ended Dec. 31, 2024, was 4.7 million Saudi riyals, compared with 5.6 million riyals earlier. EPS moved to 2.03 riyals from 2.69 riyals.

The Saudi Arabia-listed education company's revenue was 20.4 million riyals, compared with 21.2 million riyals a year ago.

data.title: Saudi Arabia Inflation Rate Accelerates to 2% in January
data.description:
The annual inflation rate in Saudi Arabia increased to 2.0% in January 2025, up from 1.9% in December 2024.

Upward price pressures came from housing and utilities (8.0% vs 8.9% in December), food and beverage prices (0.8% vs 0.8%), personal goods & services (3.3% vs 2.2%), restaurants & hotels (0.8% vs 0.9%), and education (0.6% vs 1.1).

Downward price pressure came from clothing and footwear (-1.5% vs -2.2%), furnishing (-2.4% vs -2.8%), health (-0.3% vs -1.1%), transport (-1.9% vs -2.5%), communication (-1.9% vs -1.5%), recreation and culture (-1.6% vs -1.6%), and tobacco (-0.4% vs -1.5%).

On a monthly basis, the CPI was up 0.3%, after a flat reading in the prior period.

data.title: Musk's The Boring Co., Dubai Sign Contract for Hyperloop Transport Network Deal
data.description:
Dubai's Road and Transport Authority signed a memorandum of understanding with Elon Musk's The Boring Co. for the construction of an electric vehicle transport network.

The agreement covers the construction of 11 stations for the Dubai Loop, which will span 17 kilometers, according to a Feb. 13 release.

data.title: iHit Redefines Industry Standards: 2025 North American Vape Hybrid Revolution—Hybrid-Coil Heating Technology 1+1>2, Unlocking Next-Gen Vaping Experience
data.description:
LAS VEGAS, Feb. 16, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — iHit, a global pioneer in vaping technology, unveiled its groundbreaking hybrid-powered product at the Champs Trade Show, marking the entry of the vaping industry into the “Hybrid-Coil 2.0 Era.” Centered on the philosophy of “Technology as Experience,” this release achieves the first dynamic synergy between Ceramic-Coil and Mesh-Coil technologies, powered by a ‘Hybrid-Coil Synergy + Triple-Tank Control’ architecture, redefining the performance boundaries and sensory freedom of vaping products.

Technological Revolution: From “Dual Mesh Coil” to “Hybrid-Coil Coexistence of Mesh and Ceramic”

The hybrid-powered vaping product features an internal Triple-Tank System (Nicotine/Ice/Flavor pods inside) and a dynamic interactive interface, allowing users to control via dual physical buttons:

  • Nicotine Strength: Powered by the Ceramic-Coil, it offers 3-level precision control, catering to a range of preferences from smooth throat hits to intense satisfaction.
  • Ice Intensity: Supported by the Ceramic-Coil, enabling 3-level switching for compatibility with fruit, mint, and custom ice-infused flavors.
  • Flavor Mode: Supported by the Mesh-Coil, optimizing fruits flavor and aroma profiles in real time.

Hybrid-Coil Synergy Explained

1. Ceramic-Coil — Precision Heating

  • Utilizes a micron-grade ceramic matrix + embedded heating film, excelling in the atomization of nicotine salts and ice molecules.
  • Designed for nicotine salts and ice molecules, with a minimum vapor particle size of 0.3μm, delivering a “silky throat feel, instant ice cooling sensation” experience.

2. Mesh-Coil — Flavor Enhancer

  • Features a high-density metal mesh coil, heating to 180°C instantly to break down flavor compounds.
  • Moisture-Lock Aroma Technology resolves high-temperature flavor loss, significantly enhancing vapor density and sweetness perception.
  • With a vapor particle size of 1.2μm, it combines with the ceramic-coil output to deliver a richer, multi-layered taste experience.

User Experience: One Device, Infinite Scenarios

  • Nicotine Control: Ceramic-Coil ensures precise nicotine delivery, catering to both light and strong throat hit preferences.
  • Ice Intensity: Ceramic-Coil provides consistent ice levels, enhancing the freshness of fruit and mint flavors.
  • Flavor Optimization: Mesh-Coil maximizes flavor richness and sweetness, creating a balanced and immersive vaping experience.

About iHit Tech | SMISS Group

iHit, launched by SMISS, is a health tech-focused vaporization solutions provider dedicated to revolutionizing the vaping experience through cutting-edge innovations.

Business Contact

Email: support@ihitglobal.com

Web: https://www.ihitglobal.com/

A photo accompanying this announcement is available at:

https://www.globenewswire.com/NewsRoom/AttachmentNg/88c5e018-d166-48ae-9414-69a8a266d825

data.title: National Medical Care Signs SAR92 Million Skeleton Construction Deal
data.description:
National Medical Care (SASE4005) signed a contract worth 91.8 million Saudi riyals with Construction Land for Contracting Co., according to a Sunday release.

Under the 12-month deal, the client will execute a construction skeleton for the first phase of the healthcare facility operator's Care Medical Hospital in Riyadh, Saudi Arabia.

data.title: S&P Downgrades Austria’s Outlook to Stable
data.description:
S&P Global Ratings revised Austria’s outlook to "stable" from "positive" and affirmed the credit ratings at ‘AA+/A-1+’, citing fiscal and economic challenges. “Austria's economy has been in recession for over two years, and its growth outlook remains fragile.

Real GDP contracted by about 1% in 2024 amid sluggish private consumption as well as weak investment activity, mainly mirroring the softened construction and manufacturing sectors," the rating agency said.

The GDP is expected to grow at 0.4% in real terms this year amid elevated economic risks, including those from international trade flows.

The budget deficit is estimated at around 4% of GDP, surpassing previous expectations. "Despite an ambitious budgetary consolidation program, we do not anticipate deficits declining below 3% of GDP before 2026," the agency added.

Moody's credit rating for Austria was last set at Aa1 with a stable outlook.

DBRS' credit rating for Austria was last reported at AAA with a stable outlook.

data.title: Tanmiah Food Reports Rise in FY24 Profit, Revenue
data.description:
Tanmiah Food (SASE:2281) said Sunday that profit and revenue for full-year 2024 climbed year over year.

Net profit attributable to owners for the 12 months ended Dec. 31, 2024, was 95.8 million Saudi riyals, compared with 75.9 million riyals earlier. EPS moved to 4.8 riyals from 3.8 riyals.

The Saudi Arabia-based poultry and meat products company's revenue was 2.56 billion riyals, compared with 2.09 billion riyals a year ago.

data.title: Peru Economic Activity Growth Hits 7-Month High
data.description:
Peru’s GDP advanced by 4.85% year-on-year in December 2024, up from 3.93% growth in the previous month, marking the ninth consecutive period of economic expansion.

The latest reading also marked the strongest growth since May, boosted by growth in almost all sectors.

Output accelerated for manufacturing (11.85% vs 6.67% in November) and commerce (3.64% vs 3.45%), while recovering in mining (1.97% vs -2.19%).

Meanwhile, output growth slowed in agriculture (7.54% vs 12.41%), transport (7.27% vs 7.98%), electricity, gas, and water (1.74% vs 3.11%), and accommodation and restaurants (4.54% vs 4.78%).

Additionally, construction output shrank much softer (-0.88% vs -2.40%).

For the 2024 full year, GDP grew by 3.34%, the fastest pace post-pandemic.

data.title: Emirates Reem Investments Resumes Trading in Dubai
data.description:
Emirates Reem Investments (DFM:ERC) recommenced trading on the Dubai Financial Market in the afternoon of Feb. 14, according to a same-day release.

The trading suspension, which was implemented earlier on the same day, was lifted after the Dubai-based bottled mineral water company published the results of its board meeting.

data.title: United Foods Posts Higher FY24 Profit, Revenue
data.description:
United Foods (DFM:UFC) said Feb. 14 that profit and revenue for full-year 2024 grew year over year.

Profit attributable to owners of the group for the 12 months ended Dec. 31, 2024, was 30.8 million Emirati dirhams, compared with 30.7 million dirhams earlier. EPS moved to 1.02 dirhams from 0.92 dirham.

The Dubai-listed food products company's revenue from contracts with customers was 601.7 million dirhams, compared with 589.4 million dirhams a year ago.

Meanwhile, the board proposed a dividend of 1 dirham per share. It paid 0.60 dirham a year ago.

data.title: Bellscoin (BELLS) - Final Halving - 04 May 2025
data.description:
Bellscoin’s final halving could significantly affect BELLS prices. By reducing the mining reward, the supply of new coins will decrease. This can often lead to higher prices if demand remains the same or increases, as seen in previous halvings. However, the market's expectations and current demand will play a major role. If investors anticipate high future value, BELLS might see a price rise leading up to and following the halving. On the other hand, if demand falters, the price impact might be minimal. For more details, check the source here.

Bellscoin Blockchain
@BellsChain

data.title: National Corp. for Tourism and Hotels to Halt Trading in Abu Dhabi
data.description:
National Corp. for Tourism and Hotels (ADX:NCTH) shares will temporarily pause trading on the Abu Dhabi Securities Exchange from 12 pm local time on Feb. 25 due to the meeting of the company's board of directors.

The hotel operator will resume trading after the bourse receives the meeting results, according to a Feb. 14 release.

data.title: Unikai Foods Logs Higher FY24 Net Profit, Revenue
data.description:
Unikai Foods (DFM:UNIKAI) said Feb. 14 that profit and revenue for full-year 2024 increased year over year.

Net profit for the 12 months ended Dec. 31, 2024, was 24.3 million Emirati dirhams, compared with 20.4 million dirhams in the year-ago period. EPS was flat at 0.63 dirham.

The Dubai-based consumer goods company's revenue was 403.6 million dirhams, compared with 352.7 million dirhams a year ago.

data.title: Aman Swings to Loss in FY24; Revenue Declines
data.description:
Dubai Islamic Insurance & Reinsurance (DFM:AMAN), d/b/a Aman, said Feb. 14 that it swung to a loss for full-year 2024, while revenue declined year over year.

Net loss for the 12 months ended Dec. 31, 2024, was 18.3 million Emirati dirhams, compared with a net profit of 32 million dirhams earlier. Loss per share moved to 0.081 dirham from EPS of 0.142 dirham.

The insurance company's revenue was 70 million dirhams, compared with 50.6 million dirhams a year ago.

data.title: Al Sagr National Insurance Posts FY24 Loss; Revenue Gains
data.description:
Al Sagr National Insurance (DFM:ASNIC) on Feb. 14 reported a loss for full-year 2024, while revenue increased year over year.

Net loss for the 12 months ended Dec. 31, 2024, was 114.8 million Emirati dirhams, compared with a net profit of 6.4 million dirhams earlier. Loss per share was 0.49 dirham compared with the EPS of 0.02 dirham.

The Abu Dhabi-based insurer's revenue was 976.5 million dirhams, compared with 917.3 million dirhams a year ago.

data.title: Takaful Emarat - Insurance Turns to FY24 Net Profit; Revenue Climbs
data.description:
Takaful Emarat - Insurance (DFM:TAKAFUL-EM) said Feb. 14 that it swung to a profit in full-year 2024, while revenue jumped year over year.

Net profit for the 12 months ended Dec. 31, 2024, was 1.6 million Emirati dirhams, compared with a loss of 12.4 million dirhams a year ago. EPS moved to 0.7 dirham from a loss per share of 0.08 dirham.

The United Arab Emirates-based insurance company's revenue was 420.3 million dirhams, compared with 228.7 million dirhams previously.

data.title: Gulf Navigation Turns to FY24 Loss; Revenue Falls
data.description:
Gulf Navigation (DFM:GULFNAV) said on Feb. 14 that it descended into a loss for full-year 2024 as revenue declined year over year.

Net loss for the 12 months ended Dec. 31, 2024, was 22.8 million Emirati dirhams, compared with the net profit of 21.3 million dirhams earlier. Loss per share moved to 0.017 dirham from EPS of 0.023 dirham.

The Dubai-based maritime and shipping company's gross revenue was 88.7 million dirhams, compared with 105.5 million dirhams a year ago.
